response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong systems instincts, low-latency experience at Sociomantic using DLang (asynchronous I/O, fibers, high-performance data), and demonstrated geospatial work via GISCollective and Green Map. However, the role requires modern C++ or Rust as a primary skill, and the resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments back in 2014-2015, with no Rust and no navigation/routing-tile experience. The DLang systems background and GIS exposure make this a borderline 'maybe' rather than a clear no.
strengths: Low-latency systems experience with async I/O and high-throughput services (Sociomantic)
Hands-on geospatial/GIS platform experience via GISCollective and Green Map
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, Linux, distributed systems exposure
concerns: Minimal recent C++ experience (last role ~2015) and no Rust on resume
No navigation/routing pipeline or map-tiling background
Recent work skewed to TypeScript/Node web stack, not systems-level C++/Rust
